channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50 sources.

item.title: Patient Engagement, Clinical Outcomes, and Satisfaction of an Advanced Orthopedic Virtual Physical Therapy Model: A Retrospective Cohort Study.
item.description: A retrospective cohort study found that an advanced orthopedic virtual physical therapy model achieved 62.8% evaluation completion and 70.9% care episode completion, with 96% of completers achieving Minimal Clinically Important Differences.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Early Weight-Bearing Speeds Recovery After Unstable Ankle Fracture Surgery: study
item.description: A study by Mohammadhadi Mollaabbasi et al. published in “JBJS Open Access” found that early weight-bearing (EWB) and mobilization significantly improve functional outcomes, rehabilitation progress, and complication rates following surgical fixation of unstable ankle fractures.
item.author: Medical Dialogues

item.title: Pelvic floor muscle training for pelvic organ prolapse: A systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials
item.description: This systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials evaluated the efficacy of pelvic floor muscle training (PFMT) for pelvic organ prolapse (POP).
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Effectiveness of non-pharmacological interventions for lower limb motor impairment in post-stroke hemiplegia: a systematic review and Bayesian network meta-analysis | Journal of NeuroEngineering and Rehabilitation
item.description: A systematic review and Bayesian network meta-analysis evaluated the effectiveness of non-pharmacological interventions for lower limb motor impairment in post-stroke hemiplegia.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Read more about CMS Proposes Transformational Medicare Reforms to Expand Accountable Care, Modernize Physician Payment, and Shift from Sick Care to Healthcare
item.description: CMS proposes transformational Medicare reforms to expand accountable care, modernize physician payment, and shift from sick care to healthcare.
item.author: CMS

item.title: Read more about Calendar Year (CY) 2027 Medicare Physician Fee Schedule Proposed Rule (CMS-1848-P) — Medicare Shared Savings Program Proposals
item.description: The Calendar Year (CY) 2027 Medicare Physician Fee Schedule Proposed Rule (CMS-1848-P) includes proposals related to the Medicare Shared Savings Program.
item.author: CMS

item.title: Comparative efficacy of different joint mobilization techniques in shoulder adhesive capsulitis: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis compared different non-thrust joint mobilization techniques for shoulder adhesive capsulitis, finding generally similar clinical effects on objective and subjective outcomes.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: APTA, Hartford HealthCare Launch Frailty Pilot to Advance Value-Based Care
item.description: APTA and Hartford HealthCare have launched a pilot program focusing on frailty to advance value-based care, highlighting the expanding role of physical therapy in preventive and integrated health models.
item.author: APTA

item.title: Mental health in tennis: a cross-organisational hybrid summit-Delphi consensus process
item.description: A multimethod consensus process, including an in-person summit and Delphi process, generated recommendations for mental healthcare and wellness promotion among elite tennis athletes, including juniors, involving 83 participants from diverse tennis organizations.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Incidence of injury and illness at the Paris 2024 Paralympic Games: post-pandemic trends and sport-specific risks
item.description: A prospective observational study monitored 4450 athletes during the 2024 Paris Paralympic Games, reporting 535 injuries (incidence of 8.6 per 1000 athlete days) and sport-specific risks, with Taekwondo and football 5-a-side showing the highest rates.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: GaitEncoder: A Foundation Model of Gait Kinematics for Diverse Clinical Applications and Pathologies
item.description: GaitEncoder is a generative foundation model for gait kinematics that leverages smartphone video-based gait analysis across 8 datasets (6500+ participants) for diverse clinical applications and pathologies, enabling actionable insights for personalized treatment.
item.author: medRxiv

item.title: Effects of exercise on physical function and pain in adults with rheumatoid arthritis: an exit meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials
item.description: This meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials investigated the effects of exercise on physical function and pain in adults with rheumatoid arthritis, building on previous guidelines.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Immediate Weight-Bearing and Early Ambulation Rehabilitation After Femoral Neck System Fixation for Undisplaced Femoral Neck Fractures: A Prospective Randomized Controlled Trial
item.description: A prospective randomized controlled trial investigated the clinical and functional outcomes of immediate weight-bearing and early ambulation rehabilitation after femoral neck system fixation for undisplaced femoral neck fractures.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Prehabilitation Improves Surgical Outcomes in Older Adults Undergoing Spinal Fusion: Study
item.description: A study found that prehabilitation improves surgical outcomes in older adults undergoing spinal fusion surgery, highlighting the benefits of pre-operative rehabilitation.
item.author: Medical Dialogues

item.title: Cost-effectiveness of Pilates compared with home-based exercises for people with chronic non-specific low back pain: a trial-based economic evaluation.
item.description: An economic evaluation alongside an RCT (n=145) in Brazil found Pilates to be similar to home-based exercises for chronic non-specific low back pain in terms of clinical outcomes, with cost-effectiveness still under investigation.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Acute Modulation of Lumbar Motor Unit Behavior During Experimentally Induced Low Back Pain: A Motor Unit Decomposition Analysis.
item.description: An observational study on 33 healthy participants with experimentally induced acute low back pain investigated its effects on lumbar motor unit discharge characteristics and force steadiness during spinal extension.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Exploration of lifestyle interventions for individuals with knee osteoarthritis: A scoping review.
item.description: A scoping review synthesizing 36 studies (including 29 RCTs) explored the range, characteristics, and outcome measures of lifestyle interventions for knee osteoarthritis, such as exercise and weight management.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Prevalence, Patterns, Treatment Outcomes and Costs of Physiotherapy-Managed Musculoskeletal Disorders in Nigeria.
item.description: A 10-year retrospective study of 1582 patients at a Nigerian University Teaching Hospital assessed the prevalence, patterns, treatment effectiveness, and cost of physiotherapy for musculoskeletal disorders.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Physical therapy protocols for patients with a megaprosthesis after bone tumor resection.
item.description: Physical therapy protocols were developed through an explorative literature search and two consensus procedures with orthopedic oncologic surgeons and physical therapists for patients with megaprostheses after bone tumor resection.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: In safe hands - patients with knee and hip osteoarthritis expectations and experience of physical therapist-led triage in a secondary care setting.
item.description: An explorative qualitative study in a Swedish orthopedic clinic explored the expectations and experiences of hip or knee OA patients regarding PT-led triage, using in-depth interviews.
item.author: PubMed
